scaled_oracle_cases.description: Verify that serialize correctly preserves all sections, options, and values in a round-trip, including edge-case values like empty strings, numbers, and special characters.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: The serialize function must produce a file that, when re-read with ConfigParser, exactly matches the original config object, confirming reversible serialization and handling of edge-case option values.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: Scaled case adds round-trip equality contract: the written file must parse to an identical ConfigParser object, including preservation of value types (strings) and special characters.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: serialize produces a file that either omits options, changes value formatting (e.g., escaping), or fails to handle values containing '=' or leading/trailing spaces, causing re-parsed config to differ.
scaled_oracle_cases.setup_code:
import configparser
import os
from io import StringIO

original_config = configparser.ConfigParser()
original_config['General'] = {
    'version': '1.0',
    'enabled': 'yes',
    'description': 'Main settings'
}
original_config['User'] = {
    'name': 'Alice',
    'email': 'alice@example.com',
    'role': 'admin'
}
original_config['Paths'] = {
    'input': '/data/input.txt',
    'output': '/data/output.txt',
}
# Edge-case values
original_config['Edge'] = {
    'empty': '',
    'equals': 'a=b',
    'leading_space': '  value',
    'trailing_space': 'value  ',
    'number': '42',
    'special': '#comment?',
}
path = 'test_config.ini'
scaled_oracle_cases.call_code:
result = serialize(original_config, path)
# Re-read the file and compare
parsed_config = configparser.ConfigParser()
parsed_config.read(path)
# Check that all sections and options match
sections_match = set(original_config.sections()) == set(parsed_config.sections())
options_match = True
for section in original_config.sections():
    if original_config[section] != parsed_config[section]:
        options_match = False
        break
if sections_match and options_match:
    print('Round-trip successful: written config matches original.')
else:
    print('Round-trip failed: written config differs from original.')
    # For debugging, print differences
    print('Original sections:', list(original_config.sections()))
    print('Parsed sections:', list(parsed_config.sections()))
    for section in original_config.sections():
        if original_config[section] != parsed_config[section]:
            print(f'Section {section} mismatch')
            print('Original:', dict(original_config[section]))
            print('Parsed:', dict(parsed_config[section]))

stdout_contains: Round-trip successful: written config matches original.
